target_files.description: Core scanner that needs incremental support. scanCurrentElements() always does full scan.
Existing IncrementalCache class with full implementation. Should be reused or moved to shared location.
current_issues: Only used by pipeline, not scanner

target_files.description: CLI scan command. Needs --watch flag added.
NEW FILE: File-watcher module using chokidar. Will handle watch events and trigger incremental scans.

reference_components.name: IncrementalCache
reference_components.relevance: Primary reference for cache implementation. Has all logic needed for scanner integration.

reference_components.name: PipelineOrchestrator
reference_components.relevance: Shows how to integrate IncrementalCache into a workflow. Scanner should follow similar pattern.

coding_standards: Use async/await for all file operations
Use fs/promises, not sync fs methods
Log with console.log in scanner, Logger in CLI
Follow existing error handling patterns (try/catch with console.warn)
Maintain Windows path compatibility (already normalized in scanner)
Export functions with clear JSDoc comments

risks.risk: Circular dependency between scanner and pipeline if cache moved
risks.mitigation: Either move cache to src/cache/ or scanner imports from pipeline/

risks.risk: File-watcher performance on Windows with large directories
risks.mitigation: Test with 10k+ files, may need to use native Windows APIs if chokidar slow

risks.risk: Cache corruption if process killed mid-scan
risks.mitigation: Cache saves atomically, can rebuild from scratch if corrupted

architecture_decisions.decision: Where to place IncrementalCache - keep in pipeline/ or move to cache/
architecture_decisions.reason: Cleaner architecture - cache should be shared utility, not owned by pipeline. Both scanner and pipeline can import from shared location.

architecture_decisions.decision: Should file-watcher be part of scanner.ts or separate module
architecture_decisions.recommendation: Separate module (file-watcher.ts) that imports scanner incrementally
